question: Decimetric waves correspond to the frequency range:
A radio beacon has an operational range of 10 NM. By what factor should the transmitter power be increased in order to achieve an operational range of 20 NM?
The frequency which corresponds to a wavelength of 12 cm is:
The wavelength of a radio signal with a frequency of 121.95 MHz is:
The wave length of a radio signal transmitted at a frequency of 75 MHz is:
The wave length of a radio signal transmitted at a frequency of 118.7 MHz is:
The VHF direction finder uses the following wavelengths:
The advantage of slotted antennas in modern radar technology is to…
options: produce a wide beam for better target detection.
greatly reduce lateral lobes, consequently concentrating more energy into the main beam.
eliminate the need for azimuth slaving.
simultaneously transmit weather and mapping beams.
true: greatly reduce lateral lobes, consequently concentrating more energy into the main beam.

question: Considering a primary radar system, what kind of aerials are used?
options: An omnidirectional antenna for transmitting, and a directional antenna for receiving.
A directional antenna for transmitting, and an omnidirectional antenna for receiving.
One directional antenna both for transmitting and for receiving.
One directional antenna for transmitting and one for receiving.
true: One directional antenna both for transmitting and for receiving.

question: Comparing a parabolic reflector with a flat plate antenna of the same size
options: the parabolic reflector has a considerably smaller beam width.
the parabolic reflector generates less side lobes than the flat plate antenna.
the flat plate antenna has a considerably smaller beam width.
the flat plate antenna generates less side lobes than the parabolic reflector.
true: the flat plate antenna generates less side lobes than the parabolic reflector.

question: Which aeronautical radio frequency band uses refraction within ionospheric layers?
An apparent increase in the transmitted frequency, which is proportional to the transmitter velocity, will occur when the…
options: receiver moves away from the transmitter.
transmitter moves away from the receiver.

question: Due to ‘Doppler’ effect an apparent decrease in the transmitted frequency, which is proportional to the transmitter’s velocity, will occur when:
options: the transmitter and receiver move towards each other.
the transmitter moves away from the receiver.
there is no relative movement between the transmitter and the receiver.
the transmitter moves toward the receiver.
true: the transmitter moves away from the receiver.

question: Skip distance is the…
options: wavelength distance of a certain frequency.
range from the transmitter to the first sky wave return.
true: range from the transmitter to the first sky wave return.

question: In the propagation of MF waves, the phenomenon of FADING is particularly found:
options: by day, due to the combination of sky and ground waves.
at night, due to the combination of the sky and ground waves.
true: at night, due to the combination of the sky and ground waves.

question: The skip distance of HF-transmission will increase with…
options: higher frequency and lower level of the refracting ionospheric layer.
higher frequency and higher level of the refracting ionospheric layer.
lower frequency and higher level of the refracting ionospheric layer.
lower frequency and lower level of the refracting ionospheric layer.
true: higher frequency and higher level of the refracting ionospheric layer.

question: The wavelength of a non-directional beacon (NDB) with a frequency of 300 kHz is:
With regard to types of transmission, which of the following statements is true:
options: Pulse transmission is suitable only for NDB's
Continuous Wave transmission varies only in amplitude
In pulse transmission the frequency is modulated to facilitate range measurement
Continuous Wave transmission can be either frequency or amplitude modulated
true: Continuous Wave transmission can be either frequency or amplitude modulated

question: Attenuation of radio waves means:
options: Only the absorbtion of radio energy by the sea
The atmospheric bending of the waves
The weakening of the radiated waves
Only the scattering of the waves by the Tropopause
true: The weakening of the radiated waves

question: The VHF frequency band has a wavelength limit of:
Refer to image In the diagram, which refers to the propagation of HF radio waves, the letters A, B, C, D and E correctly represent:
options: A Dead space     B Skip dist          C Sky wave          D Ground wave       E Critical angle
A Skip dist           B Dead space    C Ground wave    D Sky wave     E Critical angle
A Sky wave        B Dead space    C Ground wave    D Skip dist    E Critical angle
A Dead space     B Skip dist          C Ground wave    D Critical angle      E Sky wave
true: A Sky wave        B Dead space    C Ground wave    D Skip dist    E Critical angle

question: Modulation is:
options: The addition of a low frequency signal (tone, voice) to a high frequency carrier wave
The addition of a high frequency signal (tone, voice) to a low frequency carrier wave
Tuning receiver into the frequency of the transmitter
Elimination of disturbances for improving reception
true: The addition of a low frequency signal (tone, voice) to a high frequency carrier wave

question: The phenomenon of a change in direction of an EM-wave occurring due to a change in its speed is called:
In aviation electronic systems the so-called Doppler principle may be used in
options: VOR, MTI and the mapping mode of AWR.
VOR, GPS and MTI and the turbulence mode of AWR.
true: VOR, GPS and MTI and the turbulence mode of AWR.

question: What is the Doppler effect with reference to radio signals?
options: It is the shift in frequency of the radio wave due to the relative movement between transmitter and receiver
It is interference between the direct and the earth reflected wave.
It is fluctuations in their propagation speed.
It is the increases or decreases in the ground speed of the transmitting aircraft.
true: It is the shift in frequency of the radio wave due to the relative movement between transmitter and receiver

question: Which of the following summaries lists only directional antennae?
options: Dipole antenna, sense antenna, parabolic antenna, helical antenna.
Dipole antenna, loop antenna, parabolic antenna, slotted planar array antenna.
Sense antenna, parabolic antenna, slotted planar array antenna, helical antenna.
Loop antenna, parabolic antenna, slotted planar array antenna, helical antenna.
true: Loop antenna, parabolic antenna, slotted planar array antenna, helical antenna.

question: Given a wire fed with alternating current and a second wire parallel to, but remote from it. The result will be:
options: the first wire will radiate electro-magnetic waves into space and direct current will be induced in the second wire.
the first wire will radiate electro-magnetic waves into space but the transmitted energy will be completely absorbed by the second wire.
the first wire will radiate electro-magnetic waves into space and the second wire will double the transmission power of the first one.
The first wire will radiate electromagnetic waves into space and alternating current will be induced in the second wire
true: The first wire will radiate electromagnetic waves into space and alternating current will be induced in the second wire

question: Phase modulation
options: results in a change of amplitude of the carrier wave.
is a modulation form used in GPS where the phase of the carrier wave is reversed.
is a modulation form used in ILS where the phase of the navigation signals is changed with 90° or 150°.
results in a change of polarisation of the modulated signal.
true: is a modulation form used in GPS where the phase of the carrier wave is reversed.

question: What is meant by keying in A1A modulation
options: interrupting the carrier wave to break it into dots and dashes.
changing the amplitude of the carrier wave to add information.
adding information on the carrier wave by use of a modulating signal.
interrupting the modulating signal to break it into dots and dashes.
true: interrupting the carrier wave to break it into dots and dashes.

question: The phenomenon when a wave bends when it passes around an impenetrable obstacle is called:
From which physical phenomenon do sky waves originate?
A reason that GPS satellites use helical antennae is
options: to achieve better omni directional receiving qualities.
to improve the signal to noise ratio.
that the signal has a circular polarization.
true: that the signal has a circular polarization.

question: What describes polarization?
options: Rotation of the antenna around a fixed axis
Orientation of the antenna to the North Pole
Orientation of the plane of oscillation of the magnetic component of the ectromagnetic wave
Orientation of the plane of oscillation of the electrical component
true: Orientation of the plane of oscillation of the electrical component

question: What is the reason that DME utilises pulse pairs instead of single pulses?
options: To be able to use a separate interrogation and reply frequency, and thus to increase the number of DME stations that can transmit in the same part of the DME frequency band.
To increase the accuracy and reliability of the range measurement.
To distinguish DME transmissions from transmissions of other (radar) systems utilising single pulses.
To increase the number of aircraft that can simultaneously use the DME station to determine the DME range.
true: To distinguish DME transmissions from transmissions of other (radar) systems utilising single pulses.

question: The indication of the FROM/TO indicator of a VOR shifts from TO to FROM (or vice versa) when the value of the difference between the selected course and the measured radial passes in either direction
Radio waves travel at:
options: A speed just below the speed of sound
A speed depending on the type of electroamgetic waves

question: Which of the following is true with reference to frequency of a radio wave?
options: Frequency is the length of a radio wave expressed as an angle
Frequency is the number of cycles in one second in a radio wave expressed in Hertz (Hz)
Frequency is the minimum number of cycles occurring in one minute in a radio wave expressed in Hertz (Hz)
Frequency is the maximum deflection in a radio wave expressed in Hertz (Hz)
true: Frequency is the number of cycles in one second in a radio wave expressed in Hertz (Hz)

question: The frequency which corresponds to a wavelength of 8.25 m is:
The frequency which corresponds to a wavelength of 3 km is:
Which of the following terms describes the maximum deflection in an oscillation?
Single-sideband modulation (SSB) is a modulation technique where only one sideband is transmitted, SSB is used for:
options: HF Volmet and HF two-way communication
true: HF Volmet and HF two-way communication

question: What describes ‘Polarisation’?
options: The orientation of the plane of oscillation of the magnetic component of the electromagnetic wave.
The rotation of the antenna around a fixed axis.
The orientation of the plane of oscillation of the electrical component of the electromagnetic wave.
The orientation of the antenna to the north pole.
true: The orientation of the plane of oscillation of the electrical component of the electromagnetic wave.

question: The simplest type of antenna construction is a:
options: Dipole antenna which is a wire of length equal to one half of the wavelength
Slotted antenna used in modern weather radars
Parabolic antenna used in weather radars
Loop antenna used in ADF receivers
true: Dipole antenna which is a wire of length equal to one half of the wavelength

question: A Loop Antenna is commonly used in:
Concerning the wave propagation in the ionosphere, we denote three layers. Those three layers are:
options: D, E and F layers and their depth varies with time
A, C and E layers with equal depth
D, E and F layers and their depth does not vary with time
A, B and C layers and their depth varies with time
true: D, E and F layers and their depth varies with time

question: Which statement about VHF/UHF Frequencies is correct?
options: VHF frequencies below 120 MHz may be subjected to sporadic E-reflections and then have sky wave. For both VHF/UHF the sky wave is the main propagation path of practical use
For both VHF/UHF propagation, the space wave is the only propagation path of practial use. There is no sky wave under normal conditions
Neither VHF/UHF has a sky wave under any conditions. For VHF the surface wave is the main propagation path and for UHF the space wave
VHF has, under normal conditions, a sky wave whereas UHF has not. For both VHF/UHF propagation the space wave is the main propagation path
true: For both VHF/UHF propagation, the space wave is the only propagation path of practial use. There is no sky wave under normal conditions

question: Refer to image An amplitude modulation is shown in figure:
A radio signal may be classified by three symbols in accordance with the ITU radio regulation e.g. A1A. Which statement is true?
options: The second symbol indicates the nature of information to be transmitted
The third symbol indicates the nature of the signal modulating the main carrier
The three symbols together indicate which device is transmitting on the carrier
The first symbol indicates the type of modulation of the main carrier
true: The first symbol indicates the type of modulation of the main carrier

question: The electromagnetic waves refracted from the E and F layers of the ionosphere are called:
An electromagnetic wave consists of an oscillating electric (E) and an oscillating magnetic (H) field. Which of the following answers is true?
options: The E field and the H field are perpendicular to each other.
The H field is parallel to the wire and the E field is perpendicular to it.
When a constant current is sent through the antenna the E and H field are parallel.
A dipole antenna is only capable to transmit the H field.
true: The E field and the H field are perpendicular to each other.

question: Diffraction is the process by which:
options: A ground wave is attenuated over rough ground
A direct wave is bent around the form of the earth
Radio waves travel over and around obstacles
A space wave penetrates the ionosphere
true: Radio waves travel over and around obstacles

question: Radio waves in the VHF and higher frequency bands propagate mainly as
The phenomenon of a change in direction of an EM-wave occurring at an interface between two different media, so that the wave returns into the medium from which it originated, is called:
Single Side Band (SSB) is used
options: with transmissions of the Meteorological aerodrome Report (METAR)
in VHF transmissions to reduce transmission power.
with NDB transmissions to reduce the bandwidth

question: Modulation is:
options: The amplification of the received radio wave in the detector
The transformation of the radio wave into a digital signal
The separation of information from the radio wave during receiving
The addition of information onto a radio wave during transmission
true: The addition of information onto a radio wave during transmission

question: Reflection from ionospheric layers is used in the following radio frequencies:
The process by which EM-energy is taken up by the atmosphere is called:
Which statement about VHF/UHF propagation in connection with Ground Direction Finders is correct?
options: Neither VHF/UHF has a sky wave under any conditions. For VHF the surface wave is the main propagation path and for UHF the space wave.
For both VHF/UHF propagation, the space wave is the only propagation path of practical use. There is no sky wave under normal conditions.
VHF-frequencies below 120 MHz may be subjected to sporadic E-reflection and then have a sky wave. For both VHF/UHF the surface wave is the only propagation path of practical use.
VHF has, under normal conditions, a sky wave, whereas UHF has not. For both VHF/UHF propagation the space wave is the main propagation path.
true: For both VHF/UHF propagation, the space wave is the only propagation path of practical use. There is no sky wave under normal conditions.

question: The superposition of two EM-waves of the same or nearly the same frequency is called
Mountain effect, occurring for instance with NDBs, is caused by what physical phenomenon?
The frequency which corresponds to a wavelength of 12 cm is:
What is the definition of an antenna?
options: A wave type transducer for the process of converting a line AC into a free electromagnetic wave.
A transformer for the process of converting AC into a radio wave
A device used for the purpose of receiving a free electromagnetic radio wave and converting it into an audible frequency.
A wave type transducer for the process of converting DC into a free electromagnetic wave.
true: A wave type transducer for the process of converting a line AC into a free electromagnetic wave.

question: With a Doppler VOR system and frequency decreasing:
options: the transmitter and the receiver are moving closer
the transmitter and receiver are stationary
the transmitter is moving to the receiver

question: A vertically-mounted dipole antenna is used for RF transmissions.The receiving antenna has to be …
options: aligned horizontally in order to receive the electrical field of the RF transmission in the horizontal plane.
in the same vertical alignment in order to receive the electrical field of the RF transmission in the vertical plane.
aligned horizontally in order to receive the electrical field of the RF transmission in the vertical plane.
in the same vertical alignment in order to receive the electrical field of the RF transmission in the horizontal plane.
true: in the same vertical alignment in order to receive the electrical field of the RF transmission in the vertical plane.

question: A VHF radio signal is amplitude modulated by a low frequency signal corresponding to the pilot's voice. The spectrum of the modulated signal highlights that the useful information is located:
options: on both in the upper and lower sidebands
true: on both in the upper and lower sidebands

question: HF "Volmet" broadcasts meteorological information for aircraft in flight by using HF radio transmissions. The characteristics of the HF signal are that:
options: the carrier wave is amplitude modulated and only one of the two sidebands is transmitted (single sideband transmission)
the carrier wave is frequency modulated and only one of the two sidebands is transmitted (single sideband transmission)
the carrier wave is frequency modulated with a modulation index of 16
the carrier wave is amplitude modulated with a fixed modulation depth
true: the carrier wave is amplitude modulated and only one of the two sidebands is transmitted (single sideband transmission)

question: A radio signal is used to establish voice communications between the ground and an aircraft.  This signal is transmitted in A3E. The number "3" means that the:
options: emitter transmits the half carrier and only one of the two sidebands
carrier is modulated by an analogue signal
emitter transmits the complete carrier and the two sidebands
carrier is modulated by a digital information
true: carrier is modulated by an analogue signal

question: A radio signal is transmitted in A2A. These three symbols collectively mean:
options: an on-off keying of an amplitude modulated transmission used for aural telegraphy
an amplitude modulation used for telephony, single sideband, with suppressed carrier
an amplitude modulation used for telephony, double sideband
a frequency modulation with on-off keying telegraphy of an audio frequency
true: an on-off keying of an amplitude modulated transmission used for aural telegraphy

question: Complete the following statement: a signal of wavelength 711 m is transmitted in the (1) band and it propagates by using the (2) wave path. Which combination of answers states the correct answers for (1) and (2)?
A dipole antenna in its simplest form, suited for a frequency of 110 MHz, ought to have a length of
The sky wave is an electromagnetic wave which travels?
options: Into space to be refracted in the ionosphere
Inside the ionosphere along the boundary layer
By line of sight between transmitter and receiver
true: Into space to be refracted in the ionosphere

question: VHF propagates
options: As ground waves during the day & as ground and sky waves by night
As sky waves during the day & ground waves and sky waves by night

question: What is the simplest type of antenna called?
VLF radio waves travel...
options: during daytime as ground and sky waves, during night time as ground waves only
during day and night as ground waves
true: during day and night as ground waves

question: VHF (Very High Frequency) waves occur in which frequency range?
Which statement about the range of VHF waves is correct ?
options: It depends on the altitude of the aircraft and the elevation of the ground antenna.
Obstacles like mountains between transmitter and aircraft do not affect the range.
It does not depend on the elevation of the antenna of the transmitter.
It does not depend on the altitude of the aircraft but only on the power of the transmitter.
true: It depends on the altitude of the aircraft and the elevation of the ground antenna.

question: The wave length of a radio signal transmitted at a frequency of 118.7 MHz is:
The frequency of an Airborne Weather Radar is 9.33 GHz.What is the corresponding wavelength?
An Airborne Weather Radar transmits pulses of a wavelength of 3.2 cm.What is the corresponding frequency?
A parabolic antenna:
Due to ‘Doppler’ effect an apparent increase in the transmitted frequency will occur when:
options: the transmitter and receiver move towards each other.
the receiver moves away from the transmitter.
there is no relative movement between the transmitter and the receiver.
the transmitter moves away from the receiver.
true: the transmitter and receiver move towards each other.

question: Due to the Doppler effect an apparent decrease in the transmitted frequency, which is proportional to the transmitter and receiver’s relative velocity, will occur when…
options: there is no relative movement between the transmitter and the receiver.
the transmitter moves away from the receiver.
the transmitter moves toward the receiver.
the transmitter and receiver move towards each other.
true: the transmitter moves away from the receiver.

question: What is the skip zone?
options: the place where only the sky wave can be received.
the place where only the ground wave can be received.
the place where both ground and sky waves can be received.
the place where neither the ground waves nor the sky waves can be received.
true: the place where neither the ground waves nor the sky waves can be received.

question: The skip distance is:
options: the distance from transmitter to the maximum range of the ground wave
the distance from transmitter to earth surface up to the point where the first sky wave can be received
the distance from transmitter to an obstacle which limits the extension of the ground wave
the distance from transmitter to the maximum range of the sky wave
true: the distance from transmitter to earth surface up to the point where the first sky wave can be received

question: Refer to the annex. Which letter in the figure below indicates the Skip Distance?
Fading (LF and MF) can happen:
options: from the beginning of evening twilight until the end of morning twilight.
true: from the beginning of evening twilight until the end of morning twilight.

question: What is meant by the term 'Fading'?
options: The interference of the ground and sky wave.
The disappearance of the D-layer with beginning of the evening twilight.
An effect which can be observed only in the F-layer of the ionosphere.
An effect at RADAR which leads to ‘blind speed’.
true: The interference of the ground and sky wave.

question: Under what conditions can a VHF transmission often be received at a range considerably greater than line-of-sight?
options: The signal may be reflected from mountains between the transmitter and receiver.
A temperature inversion in the lower atmosphere can cause ‘super-refraction’.
The signal may be refracted from the ionosphere at night.
The signal may be refracted as a surface wave over a large expanse of water.
true: A temperature inversion in the lower atmosphere can cause ‘super-refraction’.

question: The unit for measuring frequency is ___, and measures cycles ___:

How is frequency modulation achieved?
options: The information is added onto the carrier wave by interrupting the carrier.
The information is added onto the carrier wave by reversing the phases of the carrier.
The information is added onto the carrier wave by altering the amplitude of the carrier.
The information is added onto the carrier wave by altering the frequency of the carrier.
true: The information is added onto the carrier wave by altering the frequency of the carrier.

question: In which frequency band may fading occur during the twilight period?
How is the unit Hertz (Hz) defined?
options: The time of one complete oscillation.
The number of electromagnetic oscillations per minute.
The number of electromagnetic oscillations per second.
The distance a radio wave travels in one second.
true: The number of electromagnetic oscillations per second.

question: Radio waves travel with an approximate velocity of:
VLF radio waves travel:
options: during day and night as ground waves
during day time as sky waves
during daytime as ground and sky waves, during night time as ground waves only
true: during day and night as ground waves

question: HFradio waves travel:
options: during daytime as ground and sky waves, during night time as ground waves only.
during daytime and at night as ground waves only.
during daytime and night time as sky waves, and the range of the ground wave is very small.
true: during daytime and night time as sky waves, and the range of the ground wave is very small.

The speed of light + velocity of the transmitter.

question: Electromagnetic waves in the LF band have a frequency spectrum of:

The superposition of radio waves with approximately the same frequency is called:

How is amplitude modulation achieved?
options: The information is added onto the carrier wave by altering the amplitude of the carrier.
The information is added onto the carrier wave by interrupting the carrier.
The information is added onto the carrier wave by altering the frequency of the carrier.
The information is added onto the carrier wave by reversing the phases of the carrier.
true: The information is added onto the carrier wave by altering the amplitude of the carrier.

question: The Ground Wave propagates as follows:
options: It travels along the earth’s surface and follows the curvature of the earth.
The ground wave is transmitted toward the earth’s surface and will be reflected.
The wave will be reflected at the ionosphere back to the ground.
true: It travels along the earth’s surface and follows the curvature of the earth.

question: The Sky Wave propagates as follows:
options: It travels between ground and D layer of the ionosphere.
It travels inside the Ionosphere along the boundary between E and F layer.
It travels along the earth’s surface.
A wave transmitted into space will be reflected back to the earth’s surface by the ionosphere.
true: A wave transmitted into space will be reflected back to the earth’s surface by the ionosphere.

question: Within which frequency band listed below are radio waves propagated as space waves?
Modulation is:
options: the transformation of the radio wave into a digital signal.
the addition of information onto a radio wave during transmission.
the amplification of the received radio wave in the detector.
the separation of information from a radio wave during receiving.
true: the addition of information onto a radio wave during transmission.

question: Modulation is:
options: the addition of a high frequency signal (tone, voice) onto a low frequency carrier wave.
the elimination of disturbances for improving the reception.
the tuning of the receiver in to the frequency of the transmitter.
the addition of a low frequency signal (tone, voice) onto a high frequency carrier wave.
true: the addition of a low frequency signal (tone, voice) onto a high frequency carrier wave.

question: Modulation is
options: the process to provide a radio carrier wave for transmission.
to allow the signal to be amplified to the required power level.
the process of impressing and transporting information by radio waves.
a continuous wave which is capable of carrying audible signals without modification.
true: the process of impressing and transporting information by radio waves.

question: The function of the antenna in a radio communication system is to…
options: prevent the receiver from demodulating background noise.
act as a transducer between free-space propagation and transmission line propagation.
separate the baseband signal from the carrier wave signal.
split the electromagnetic field into separate E (Electric) and H (Magnetic) waves for transmission.
true: act as a transducer between free-space propagation and transmission line propagation.

question: What type of antenna is used in a modern Airborne Weather Radar?
When raising the frequency of an electromagnetic wave, the:
What is required to enable an antenna to transmit an electromagnetic wave?
options: The antenna has to be fed with an alternating current of high frequency.
The antenna has to be fed with a direct current.
The length of the antenna has to be equal to the wavelength of the electromagnetic wave.
The antenna has to be mounted perpendicular to the ground.
true: The antenna has to be fed with an alternating current of high frequency.

question: Under what conditions can VHF voice communications often suffer interference from transmissions on the same frequency at a distance considerably greater than line-of-sight?
options: A temperature inversion in the lower atmosphere can cause ‘super-refraction’.
The signal may be refracted as a surface wave over a large expanse of water.
The signal may be refracted from the ionosphere at night.
The signal may be refracted from mountains between the transmitter and receiver.
true: A temperature inversion in the lower atmosphere can cause ‘super-refraction’.

true: A = refraction B = absorption C = reflection

Describe the propagation of VHF radio waves:
options: During daytime as ground waves at night as sky waves
During day and night time as ground waves

question: The phase angle of a wave is:
options: The angle of the modulation on a wavelength expressed in degrees from 000° to 360°.
The deflection on a wave expressed in degrees from 000° to 360°.
The fraction of one wavelength expressed in degrees from 000° to 360°.
The position of a modulation on a wavelength expressed in degrees from 000° to 360°.
true: The fraction of one wavelength expressed in degrees from 000° to 360°.

Speed of light divided by frequency.

options: orientation of the plane of oscillation of the magnetic component of the electromagnetic wave.
rotation of the antenna around a fixed axis
orientation of the plane of oscillation of the electrical component of the electromagnetic wave.
orientation of the antenna to the North Pole.
